skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini image models through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Use Gemini image models for text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input, and grounded visual prompts.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run the infsh CLI and related install commands. The network findings are expected for image generation, while the weak cryptography findings are false positives with no cryptographic use found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Sends User Content to Image Services
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill allows Bash(infsh *) and shows infsh app run commands. Prompts, image URLs, and input files may be sent to inference.sh and Google Gemini services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and match the skill purpose. The risk is data exposure to external services, not hidden malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Skill Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The documentation recommends npx skills add commands for related packages. Running these commands can install additional community content outside this reviewed skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install commands are present in documentation and can change the user's local skill set. They appear informational, but execution expands trust beyond this audit.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation and Image URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a displayed image, and an example input image. No credential or data exfiltration pattern was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or sample content. I found no evidence that secrets are collected or transmitted by hidden code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static findings at the reported lines do not show cryptographic functions or weak algorithms. They are plain skill description, prompt text, and table content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines contain no hashing, encryption, or checksum logic. The high-severity cryptography alerts are unsupported by the file content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permitted External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands. This is required for functionality, but it should be disclosed because it executes a local CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly confirm external command execution. The commands are scoped to infsh.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Backed Image Generation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow depends on external image generation services and may process prompts, URLs, and uploaded image data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill describes inference.sh and Google Gemini image apps. The data sharing risk follows from the documented service calls.

content.value_statement: Image teams need fast access to Gemini image models without writing API code. This skill provides ready CLI workflows for generation, editing, and grounded visual prompts.
actual_capabilities: Runs Gemini image generation apps through the infsh CLI.
Creates images from text prompts using Gemini image models.
Edits images by passing input image URLs to supported models.
Requests multiple images from Gemini 2.5 Flash Image.
Sets aspect ratio and resolution options when supported.
Enables Google Search grounding for current visual prompts.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends prompts and image inputs to external services.
Model availability and pricing depend on inference.sh.
Generated images may require review for policy and rights issues.

use_cases.title: Concept Art Drafts
use_cases.description: Generate fast visual options from a creative brief before commissioning final artwork.

use_cases.title: Product Image Variations
use_cases.description: Create alternative compositions, ratios, and styles for campaign testing.

use_cases.title: Image Edit Prototypes
use_cases.description: Test prompt-based edits on supplied image URLs before using a production design workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to generate one image of [subject] in [style] with [mood].

prompt_templates.title: Generate Several Concepts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini 2.5 Flash Image to create four options for [project]. Keep the prompt focused on [brand or theme].

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to edit this image URL: [url]. Change [specific element] while preserving [important detail].

prompt_templates.title: Ground a Current Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini 3 Pro Image with Google Search grounding to visualize [current topic] as [art direction].

output_examples.input: Generate a product hero image for a ceramic travel mug.
output_examples.output: A polished product image with a ceramic mug, clean lighting, neutral props, and a social-ready composition.

output_examples.input: Edit a landscape image by adding a rainbow.
output_examples.output: An edited image that preserves the original landscape while adding a natural rainbow in the sky.

output_examples.input: Create four logo concepts for a coffee shop.
output_examples.output: Four distinct minimalist logo directions with different shapes, typography moods, and visual balance.

best_practices: Keep prompts specific about subject, style, lighting, and composition.
Avoid sending private images unless external processing is approved.
Use lower-cost models for drafts before requesting high-resolution output.
anti_patterns: Do not include secrets or private customer data in prompts.
Do not assume model output is legally cleared for commercial use.
Do not run related skill install commands without reviewing their source.

faq.question: What does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the inference.sh CLI to run Google Gemini native image models.

faq.question: Does it require a login?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documentation requires infsh login before running image apps.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports image editing when a model receives one or more input image URLs.

faq.question: Can it create multiple images?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show num_images for generating several outputs.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential images?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and images may be processed by external services.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs external CLI commands and uses network services for image generation.
